about.bio.1: My curiosity operates well beyond any single discipline. Whether I'm navigating the complexity of a regulated product program, investing in real estate, or engineering a livable interior space, my mind defaults to systems thinking—isolating root causes, defining constraints, and designing toward validated outcomes. Ambiguity doesn't slow me down; it accelerates my drive to learn.
about.bio.2: Ultimately, it's my ability to translate complex technical and regulatory requirements into clear, compliant, human-centered experiences—and communicate that work with precision—that defines how I lead.
about.quote.text: Exceptional design in regulated environments starts with rigorous empathy and ends with validated, measurable impact on the people who depend on it.
about.title: A systems thinker who leads at the intersection of design, R&D, and compliance.
about.value.0.desc: Every program begins with a genuine drive to understand the use environment, the end user, and the regulatory landscape before a single interface decision is made.
about.value.0.title: Use-Environment First
about.value.1.desc: In regulated product development, the smallest missed detail in a user interface can create cascading risk. Precision in documentation, validation, and design intent is non-negotiable.
about.value.1.title: Detail-Driven by Necessity
about.value.2.desc: Navigating quality, timelines, cost, and regulatory requirements simultaneously demands a leader who can negotiate outcomes with clarity and earn trust across R&D, Regulatory, Clinical, and Operations.
about.value.2.title: Cross-Functional Negotiator
about.value.3.desc: Design that cannot be measured cannot be defended. Every program I lead is tied to validated outcomes—task completion, use-error reduction, adoption rate, and delivery velocity.
about.value.3.title: Outcome-Validated

modal.project.0.challenge: Within four months of validated deployment, insights generated by this AI-driven program directly informed targeted engagement strategies that reversed a negative profit trajectory. The program moved from a –17% operating margin to a +2% margin—demonstrating how rigorous design research, agentic AI modeling, and validated UX program leadership can drive measurable, compliance-aligned commercial outcomes.

modal.project.1.category: Design Systems & Common Principles · Plexus Worldwide
modal.project.1.challenge: Architected and directed delivery of a comprehensive UX/UI design system to unify a fragmented multi-platform product experience spanning marketing, sales, consumer-facing interfaces, and internal communications tools. The program objective was not cosmetic renewal—it was a durable, validated system establishing common design principles, visual consistency across product families, and execution standards that aligned directly with regulated business and quality requirements.
modal.project.1.kpi.0.label: Reduction in Program Delivery Time
modal.project.1.kpi.1.label: Designers Across Programs
modal.project.1.kpi.2.label: Increase in Design Deliverables
modal.project.1.kpi.3.label: Designer Hours Saved (Value)
modal.project.1.outcome.0: Common design principles established across product families—eliminating inconsistencies between programs, platforms, and teams.
modal.project.1.outcome.1: Shared source of truth for all design artifacts clarifying scope, deliverables, and validation expectations across multi-disciplinary R&D teams.
modal.project.1.outcome.2: UX/UI strategy aligned to quality and business standards by embedding validated design intent directly into system governance.
modal.project.1.outcome.3: Increased design team autonomy and delivery confidence through clear, repeatable, validated patterns.
modal.project.1.outcome.4: Executive trust secured through visible, consistent design standards referenced across all program deliverables organization-wide.
modal.project.1.outcomes.heading: Key Outcomes
modal.project.1.overview: Delivered as a hybrid greenfield and progressive migration, the system lives in Figma and is referenced directly in Contentful—enabling designers, engineers, and leaders to execute from a shared, validated source of truth. Technology transfer into production achieved without regression. Adoption improved delivery velocity 50%, elevated design quality 80%, and transformed the UX/UI function from a reactive service into a scalable, strategic R&D capability.

modal.project.1.quote.text: This new system changed how we work. Delivery time was cut by roughly 50%. Visual and interaction quality jumped by about 80%. We went from weeks assembling concepts to delivering clean, standardized prototypes in days. It raised the bar for every project.

modal.project.2.challenge: Defined the end-to-end UX/UI strategy and validated interaction design for a new regulated membership onboarding and advancement experience. The core program challenge was twofold: designing compliant, frictionless flows for new and existing end-users joining in accordance with regulatory requirements, and ensuring prospective users clearly understood the value proposition without confusion, use error, or trust erosion.
modal.project.2.kpi.0.label: Target NPS Improvement
modal.project.2.kpi.1.label: End-User Touchpoints Redesigned
modal.project.2.kpi.2.label: Projected Use-Error & Churn Reduction
modal.project.2.kpi.3.label: Target Program Completion
modal.project.2.outcome.0: Clarified end-user value proposition, reducing hesitation and use errors at tier selection and onboarding decision points.
modal.project.2.outcome.1: Improved trust and transparency in regulated transaction flows through validated interaction design and guided success states.
modal.project.2.outcome.2: Subscription-led activation enabled as a first-class, validated experience—not an afterthought appended to the regulated flow.
modal.project.2.outcome.3: Reduced friction for users advancing toward participation tiers through validated, compliance-aligned interaction redesign.
modal.project.2.outcome.4: Established a scalable, compliant onboarding and advancement framework aligned to long-term retention, quality, and growth objectives.
modal.project.2.outcomes.heading: Key Outcomes
modal.project.2.overview: This program reframes onboarding as a validated, guided progression rather than a transactional compliance gate—aligning interaction design, regulatory requirements, and subscription mechanics across landing pages, cart, and checkout. Designed to reduce use errors at the most critical conversion moments, improve regulatory compliance fidelity, and support sustainable growth through a validated, scalable UX/UI foundation.

Early analysis shows faster completion times, higher subscription attachment at join, and improved confidence—an update that can potentially support both revenue and long-term success.

modal.project.4.challenge: Directing the end-to-end UX/UI refactoring of a high-volume transaction experience facing critical use-error patterns and extreme task abandonment exceeding 80%. Declining end-user trust and interaction flows diverging significantly from validated usability standards positioned this as a regulated business recovery program—not a cosmetic interface refresh.

modal.project.4.outcome.1: Reduced end-user effort in regulated transaction flows by eliminating unnecessary steps, ambiguity, and identified use-error sources.
modal.project.4.outcome.2: Subscription activation enabled as a first-class, validated interaction directly within the primary transaction flow.
modal.project.4.outcome.3: Visual consistency and interaction parity with validated usability standards—closing the gap identified in formative evaluation.
modal.project.4.outcome.4: Measurable improvement in validated retention, trust, and task completion metrics through isolated, data-driven UX/UI program changes.

modal.project.4.overview: Restoring end-user trust, reducing cognitive load and use errors, and enabling validated revenue-positive behaviors directly within the transaction experience. Partnering with executive leadership, PMO, Engineering, and Quality to establish a design-led, validated program roadmap that aligns interaction decisions with regulatory compliance, business outcomes, and quality standards.

modal.project.5.challenge: Directing the experience strategy and validated UX/UI design for a new AI-powered intelligent search system to address a critical end-user trust and result accuracy failure. Legacy search returned inaccurate results, forcing users to rely on external engines to locate products—directly undermining task completion, use confidence, and validated end-user engagement.
modal.project.5.kpi.0.label: AI-Validated Search Results
modal.project.5.kpi.1.label: Result Accuracy Rate
modal.project.5.kpi.2.label: Validated Engagement Growth
modal.project.5.kpi.3.label: Program Kickoff
modal.project.5.outcome.0: Restore end-user trust in on-platform discovery by prioritizing intent-matched, validated results in the highest-visibility interaction positions.
modal.project.5.outcome.1: Reduce use errors and end-user effort by guiding ambiguous searches toward validated outcomes rather than failing silently.
modal.project.5.outcome.2: Accelerate validated paths to transaction and subscription through intelligent, human-factors-informed product surfacing logic.
modal.project.5.outcome.3: Unify discovery across products, content, support, and resources into a single, coherent, validated experience layer.
modal.project.5.outcome.4: Establish search as a validated, strategic platform capability and retention lever—aligned with regulatory, compliance, and growth objectives.
modal.project.5.outcomes.heading: Key Outcomes
modal.project.5.overview: Search is a core end-user experience layer—not a utility. By combining deterministic relevance logic with a custom AI API integration and validated usability engineering principles, the new system is designed to deliver exact, high-confidence results, guide users through uncertainty, and surface validated recommendations that accelerate task completion, subscription, and retention outcomes.
